mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market deterioration signals severe economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which forces equity markets to reprice downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in more than 60% of triggered cases over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions do not lead to negative 5-day returns during tightening cycles, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.5% monthly are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, invalidating the inflation-risk premium mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, signals underlying demand fragility that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 50% of triggered instances in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month unemployment rate jump exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate portfolio deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a 0.5pp UNRATE jump, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
